votes.rationale: Motion Factory v0 was used to execute the full governed motion lifecycle on motion-0056 itself. The workflow — intent, context, draft, review, revise, council:run, ratify, commit — completed successfully using existing factory commands with no new code. The placeholder-first operator convention is stated as a non-enforced default workflow that replaces manual 9-file creation.

The full-cycle proof is honest about its scope: it proves the workflow executes end-to-end on one real motion, not that the factory handles all future edge cases. The placeholder-first convention is correctly framed as an operator convention without enforcement gates. Self-referential proof is acceptable because every step was human-reviewed. No code changes were needed.

Motion Factory v0 is proven end-to-end. The factory bootstrap sequence from motion-0052 through motion-0056 is internally consistent: context, deterministic scaffold, API narrative generation, atomic revision, and now full-cycle proof. The placeholder-first convention establishes a practical workflow default without overreaching into enforcement. The factory remains a bounded drafting assistant with no governance authority.
